content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Education & Degree Verification - Office of the Registrar

  - url: https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/

  - source: UC Berkeley Office of the Registrar

  - domain: registrar.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Education & Degree Verification


  The Office of the Registrar describes two different verification categories on this
  section of the UC Berkeley site.


  ## Verify Attendance and Degrees


  This category covers enrollment verification for current students and verification
  of attendance and degrees for students, alumni, and former students. The detailed
  Verify Attendance and Degrees page contains the procedures and service options for
  these records.


  ## Verify Class Enrollment


  This is a different process. It concerns how current students confirm participation
  in their classes through CalCentral and applies to undergraduate class-participation
  requirements. It is not the same as an employer or other third party checking a
  student''s degree or attendance history.


  ## Verification Support and Services


  Many verification services depend on the student having been officially registered.
  Official registration generally requires enrollment in courses, payment of at least
  20 percent of tuition and registration fees, and no registration blocks.


  UC Berkeley provides separate information about how names appear on records and
  rosters, name changes, and notarization of enrollment or degree verifications.


  A limited number of special circumstances use other procedures. Someone who urgently
  needs proof of graduation before the degree has been posted may need the expedited-degree
  information linked from the Official Transcripts page. Dean''s certifications and
  letters of good standing also use separate request instructions.


  For customized verification documents or specialized third-party forms that require
  information beyond standard official verification, the Registrar may charge a per-document
  or delivery fee. People with access may submit many such requests through CalCentral
  by opening the eForms Center and selecting the attendance-and-degrees verification
  option.


  This overview identifies the verification categories and routes readers to the detailed
  service pages. It does not itself provide every provider-specific instruction for
  an employer or other external requester.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Education & Degree Verification - Office of the Registrar
url: https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Verify Attendance and Degrees - Office of the Registrar

  - url: https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/verify-attendance-and-degrees/

  - source: UC Berkeley Office of the Registrar

  - domain: registrar.berkeley.edu


  ## Content


  # Verify Attendance and Degrees


  Most verification services can be requested online by students or former students
  who still have access to CalCentral.


  ## Enrollment Verification for Current Students


  UC Berkeley provides current students with a free, secure self-service enrollment-verification
  option through the National Student Clearinghouse. Depending on timing and eligibility,
  students may view, print, or download an advanced-registration certificate, an enrollment-verification
  certificate, a good-student-discount certificate, enrollment history, and information
  about student-loan deferments.


  Current students reach self-service enrollment verification through CalCentral under
  My Academics, Academic Records, and Enrollment Verification. Verification data is
  refreshed weekly during the add/drop adjustment period and monthly afterward; summer
  data is refreshed at least once per session.


  ## Verification of Attendance and Degrees


  UC Berkeley also produces its own Verification of Attendance and Degrees on university
  letterhead. It carries the University seal and the facsimile signature of the University
  Registrar. The university does not charge a fee for this service.


  Current students may request or download this verification through CalCentral. Alumni
  and former students who retain access may use the relevant form in CalCentral. People
  who no longer have CalCentral access may complete the Registrar''s request form
  and submit it in person at Cal Student Central, by mail to the Office of the Registrar,
  or by email to Verify-Attendance-Degrees@berkeley.edu.


  Students and alumni may alternatively obtain a degree or attendance verification
  online through the National Student Clearinghouse. The Clearinghouse charges a fee
  for verifications.


  The Registrar does not complete ordinary third-party forms when the requested information
  is already supplied through Berkeley''s Verification of Attendance and Degrees or
  its enrollment-verification certificates. These official verifications may include
  degree information, dates of attendance, enrollment status, academic career, program
  or major, anticipated graduation date, and related registration information.


  ## Employers and Other Parties Outside UC Berkeley


  UC Berkeley has contracted with the National Student Clearinghouse, a nonprofit
  organization serving higher education, to verify attendance and degrees for Berkeley
  students and alumni when the request comes from employers, service providers, or
  other third parties not affiliated with UC Berkeley.


  The Clearinghouse can be contacted by phone at 703-742-4200 and charges a fee for
  verifications.


  Questions about Berkeley degree and enrollment verifications may be directed to
  Verify-Attendance-Degrees@berkeley.edu.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Verify Attendance and Degrees - Office of the Registrar
url: https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/verify-attendance-and-degrees/

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Verify Now | National Student Clearinghouse

  - url: https://www.studentclearinghouse.org/verify/

  - source: National Student Clearinghouse

  - domain: studentclearinghouse.org


  ## Content


  # Instantly Verify Student Credentials


  The National Student Clearinghouse describes itself as a nationwide source for education
  verification. It offers online verification of U.S. degree and attendance records,
  services for high-volume requesters, and around-the-clock access.


  ## Current Enrollment


  The current-enrollment service checks whether a student is presently enrolled at
  a postsecondary institution. The page lists a price for each confirmed verification
  and links to an online verification request as well as a list of participating EnrollmentVerify
  schools.


  ## Degrees and Attendance


  The Degrees & Attendance service checks whether a person attended a postsecondary
  institution or received one or more degrees from that institution. The page lists
  a base price per confirmed verification, notes that a school surcharge may also
  apply, and links to an online request flow and a participating-school list.


  ## High School Diplomas


  A separate service verifies whether a person received a high school diploma. It
  has its own pricing and participation information.


  The Clearinghouse notes that its verification services do not provide copies of
  degrees or diplomas. The service confirms credential or attendance information rather
  than delivering the underlying academic document.


  The page includes support resources and options for businesses and educational institutions
  that need additional information about verification services.


  This page describes the Clearinghouse''s services and general capabilities. It does
  not, by itself, state which specific verification provider any particular university
  has formally designated for one of its records processes.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Verify Now | National Student Clearinghouse
url: https://www.studentclearinghouse.org/verify/

The page is a **Berkeley-recognized verification route**.

UC Berkeley’s Office of the Registrar states that it has contracted with the **National Student Clearinghouse (NSC)** to verify attendance and degrees for employers and other external third parties. The service you were sent—**Degrees & Attendance** at `studentclearinghouse.org`—matches that purpose.

### Which route to use

- **For your employer’s verification request:** use the **National Student Clearinghouse – Degrees & Attendance** route they provided.
- **Do not use “Current Enrollment”** unless your employer specifically needs to know whether you are currently enrolled.
- **Do not order a transcript** unless the employer requests an academic transcript rather than a degree verification.
- If you want your own official proof, Berkeley also offers a university-issued **Verification of Attendance and Degrees** through CalCentral or the Registrar. Berkeley says that service is free, while NSC may charge a verification fee.

Berkeley’s official instructions are here:  
[Verify Attendance and Degrees — UC Berkeley Registrar](https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/verify-attendance-and-degrees/)

The NSC page you received is here:  
[National Student Clearinghouse — Verify Now](https://www.studentclearinghouse.org/verify/)

Before entering sensitive information, ensure the browser address is exactly on `studentclearinghouse.org` and that the request is specifically for **Degrees & Attendance**.

final_answer:
The page is a **Berkeley-recognized verification route**.

UC Berkeley’s Office of the Registrar states that it has contracted with the **National Student Clearinghouse (NSC)** to verify attendance and degrees for employers and other external third parties. The service you were sent—**Degrees & Attendance** at `studentclearinghouse.org`—matches that purpose.

### Which route to use

- **For your employer’s verification request:** use the **National Student Clearinghouse – Degrees & Attendance** route they provided.
- **Do not use “Current Enrollment”** unless your employer specifically needs to know whether you are currently enrolled.
- **Do not order a transcript** unless the employer requests an academic transcript rather than a degree verification.
- If you want your own official proof, Berkeley also offers a university-issued **Verification of Attendance and Degrees** through CalCentral or the Registrar. Berkeley says that service is free, while NSC may charge a verification fee.

Berkeley’s official instructions are here:  
[Verify Attendance and Degrees — UC Berkeley Registrar](https://registrar.berkeley.edu/academic-records/education-degree-verification/verify-attendance-and-degrees/)

The NSC page you received is here:  
[National Student Clearinghouse — Verify Now](https://www.studentclearinghouse.org/verify/)

Before entering sensitive information, ensure the browser address is exactly on `studentclearinghouse.org` and that the request is specifically for **Degrees & Attendance**.
